svg.title: Hip Roof — Slope Views & Framing
svg.text: RING BEAM 27'×45' — 4"×2"×3 mm HSS @ wall top
6" × 1.2 mm GI PANI PATTI (outer eave band)
SECTION A–A : TRANSVERSE (perpendicular to ridge) — TRUSS PROFILE
Fink × 3 — 27' × 6' 9" rise, bottom chord on ring beam
Ring beam at wall top (24" above eave)
SECTION B–B : LONGITUDINAL (along ridge) — TRUSS LOCATIONS
MAIN SLOPES — W & E (trapezoid, identical pair)
FRAMING DETAIL — Metal Pipe Cross Sections
6" × 1.2 mm GI strip (bottom flush with rafter bottom)

g.text: MEMBRANE — depression pressed into gap so tile lip seats
CEILING TILE — Nutical Plain 12×8 (between purlins, tabs curl over purlin tops)
EAVE ASSEMBLY — Pani Patti (GI water-protector) + 1"×1"×3 mm L-channel (L-channel top aligned to purlin top)
drawn slope-flat for clarity • actual pitch 26.0° (main slope)

svg.text: FINK TRUSS ELEVATION — bottom chord on ring beam (× 3 identical trusses)
2 diagonals from N ridge endpoint to N eave corners
2 diagonals from S ridge endpoint to S eave corners
Water-protector strip along entire eave perimeter
1"×1"×3mm angle on top of Pani Patti
N/A for hip roof (no free edges); include if adjacent to non-adjoining section
Only if roof width isn't a clean tile multiple (supports cut top tiles)
Doubled — 2 legs along each of the 4 hip ridges to support cut ceiling tiles
Fink truss — 2 sloping top chords per truss × 3 trusses
Central vertical post — peak to bottom-chord centre
Peak to bottom-chord panel points (2 per truss)
Top-chord panel points to bottom-chord panel points (2 per truss)
Perimeter tie at wall top (27' × 45' frame, 24" above eave)
3 per hip end (× 2 ends) — corner truss to N/S wall; extended through 2 ridge-zone bay(s) — continuous N wall → S wall
• Main slope pitch: 27.4° | Hip-end pitch: 39.1° | Ridge height above eave h = 9'
• Slope L (perpendicular height on the slope face): main = 19' 7", hip end = 14' 4"
Rafters — per slope, rafter count = floor(eave / spacing) + 1; each rafter's length
is the perpendicular distance from its position on the eave up to either the top edge
(central ridge, on the trapezoidal main slopes) or to a hip ridge (on the triangular
hip ends, and near the corners of the main slopes).
Purlins — placed every 12" starting 12" above the eave (the y=0 row is the eave-edge
pipe, counted separately). At height y, each purlin spans between the two hip ridges:
L(y) = eave − 2·d_hip·y/height (for the triangular hip ends, d_hip = eave/2).
Hip ridges — 4 pieces (one per corner). Each is the 3-D diagonal from a ridge
endpoint to an eave corner: L = √((eave_x_east−eave_x_west)/2)² + d_hip² + h²)
Eave edge — one continuous run around the eave bounding box:
CONSOLIDATED PROCUREMENT LIST — Totals by Material Spec
Truss top chords (6× 90' 7"), Truss bottom chords (3× 81')
Truss king posts (3× 20' 3"), Truss web diagonals (6× 57' 3"), Truss web verticals (6× 20' 3")
Roof surface area (measured on the slope face)
Main slope (trapezoid): ½·(52' + 21')·19' 7" = 715 sft × 2 faces = 1431 sft
Hip end (triangle): ½·34' 10"·14' 4" = 249 sft × 2 faces = 499 sft
Total roof surface = 2060 sft | with 10% waste allowance = 2266 sft
Items procured — for the contractor to reconcile against site delivery
1.5 tiles/sft. 12" dim spans purlin-centre to purlin-centre.
• Ridge tiles: sold per 1 running foot of ridge.
ACTION — order 34 additional running feet of ridge cap:
• Order was originally sized for the earlier two-face gable design,
which had a longer central ridge but no hip ridges. Moving to hip
shortened the central ridge (55'→15') but added 4 hip ridges of
~26' each. The 100 ft order is short by that delta.
• Tile margins (+1,136 (+38% margin), +1,300 (+38% margin)) also stem from the
earlier larger gable surface. Confirm with contractor whether the
surplus is for outbuildings or should be treated as excess.
• RIDGE: sealed with Top Flex UV tape, then ridge tiles laid without
cement mortar — the sealed tape holds them and stays watertight.
• VALLEY: 3" gap held between the two tile fields (straight-line cut).
Metal strip + cement seal the gap and channel water down; paint to match.
• BARGE: sealed with cement mortar for clean, waterproof edges.
• WALL JUNCTIONS: self-adhesive easy-flash ~5" up the wall and 5" onto
the tile; silicone bead along the top edge of the flashing.
Silicone at every gap around pipe penetrations.
• WATER CHANNELS: brackets sit in the gap above the Pani Patti, PVC
channels drop into the brackets, downpipe carries water to ground.
• NEVER step between two ceiling tiles — the tile has low strength on
its own and will crack; step only where a purlin is underneath.
• Check every ceiling tile for cracks BEFORE the membrane goes on —
a broken tile is hard to reach once membrane + top tile are down.
• Anchor bolts embedded 6–8" into footing; deeper footing = more stability.
• Column base plate welded onto the projecting rebar (2" projection)
• Flood-test the whole roof at the end for leaks. Do NOT walk on wet
tiles — they are extremely slippery when wet.
• Roof pitch: main slopes 27.4°, hip ends 39.1° (both above 20° min).
• Hip-end pitch is shallow for Konkan monsoon intensity. Give flashing
and underlay at the four hip ridges extra attention.
